title: Execution recovery after failed migration
description: A major data migration failed midway through. 40% of customers are on the new system, 60% on the old. Build the recovery plan.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at QueryDock, a Series C data platform company (390 employees, $44.7M ARR). Your immediate assignment is execution recovery after failed migration. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Optimize for repeatable outcomes over one-off wins that do not scale.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 87114
New logo adds: 1690
Activation rate: 40%
30-day retention: 36%
NPS: 30 (n=255)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 30%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: -3 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 6 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Grant Nguyen): "Buyers are asking for proof points, not intent. We need visible traction fast."
VP Engineering (Farah Foster): "Unresolved platform dependencies make estimates look precise but wrong."
Head of Customer Success (Anya Rivera): "Escalations spike after handoff because expectations are set too optimistically."
Commercial note: 3 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 17 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Keystone Legal (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Northgate Logistics (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Willow Energy (Director of Product): "Give us a narrower path that works consistently and we will scale internally."
Data gap: support transcript tagging is inconsistent across segments.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 27
Median cycle time: 20 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 56%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 1
Release rollback events last quarter: 0
Average time to resolve escalations: 29 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Describe how you will sequence work under capacity and dependency constraints.
submissionFields.label: Execution Approach

submissionFields.hint: Rank the work and defend why this order is operationally realistic.
submissionFields.label: Priority Order

submissionFields.hint: Identify cross-team dependencies, owners, and failure points.
submissionFields.label: Critical Dependencies

submissionFields.hint: Call out scope cuts and explain why each is acceptable.
submissionFields.label: What You Will Not Do

submissionFields.hint: Define rituals, escalation triggers, and decision logs.
submissionFields.label: Operating Cadence

rubric.name: Sequencing Rigor
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is the order of work realistic given dependencies and capacity?

rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are ownership, escalation paths, and cadences clear?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Does the plan proactively reduce delivery and quality risk?

rubric.name: Scope Discipline
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are cuts and boundaries explicit and maintained?

expertSolution.content: Recommendation: execute dependency-first. With 1 unresolved dependencies and 27 open P1/P2 issues, sequencing feature work before blocker removal will likely repeat prior slippage patterns.

Priority 1 is reliability and defect containment tied to 17 monthly escalations. Priority 2 is instrumentation to validate impact. Priority 3 is highest-reach workflow improvements. Priority 4 is lower-leverage enhancements. This order improves delivery confidence.

Critical dependencies include platform APIs, reporting pipelines, and security review SLA. Assign a single accountable owner per dependency and publish fallback paths. Current cycle time (20 days) and on-time rate (56%) require tighter dependency governance.
Cut speculative platform investments and low-reach custom requests this quarter. The opportunity cost is acceptable relative to unresolved risk and the need to stabilize core execution quality.
Run twice-weekly execution reviews, weekly risk checkpoints, and a persistent decision log. Farah Foster owns blocker escalations and Anya Rivera validates customer-impact closure each week.
